violations.text: The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the high temp dish machine. PIC ordered thermo labels at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Several cardboard boxes observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap provided at the time of this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels provided at the time of this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (coffee pots) were observed with an accumulation debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

(Chemicals, cleaning products) were observed stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items in the kitchen. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Cases of bread observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Staff moved the bread during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
Cleaned and sanitized utensils were not stored in a clean and dry location. Utensils will have to washed, rinsed, and sanitized again and properly put away. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The top of front chest freezer observed in poor repair. The cover observed cracked and preventiing the door to tightly close. Freezer door shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the inside of the all refrigeration units and areas underneath/behind all kitchen equipment have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Several cardboard boxes observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

At the time of inspection, cheese and coney island sauce in the back 2 door reach-in cooler were observed held at 47 - 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Food item(s) prepared on site previous day were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

At the time of inspection, the ambient air temperature measuring devices in refrigeration were observed to be inaccurate. Ambient air temperature measuring devices shall be maintained in good repair and be accurate within the intended range of use. PIC is to replace internal temperature measuring devices that are not indicating accurate internal temperature of refrigeration. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2024

The back room 2 kitchen area 2 door reach-in cooler was observed to be in poor repair/in need of adjustment. - The unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 53-56 degrees Fahrenheit - Gaskets were observed to be hanging/detached preventing the doors from closing properly At the time of inspection, the PIC called for replacement refrigeration. 2 door reach-in cooler is not to be used for time/temperature control for safety foods until adjusted/repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Facility is lacking thermo-labels or a high temp dish machine testing device. In hot water mechanical WAREWASHING operations, an irreversible registering temperature indicator shall be provided and readily accessible for measuring the UTENSIL surface temperature. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Refrigeration internal/external surfaces - Surfaces along the cookline New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
An allergy statement is needed on the menu/counter. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023

Various foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023
The back refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/23/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3-bay. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of equipment have an accumulation of dust, grease, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023
